articles.title: Bihar Cabinet Expansion: Nitish Kumar’s son Nishant among 32 takes oath as ministers in Samrat Choudhary-led cabinet at Gandhi Maidan| Watch
articles.description: The cabinet of Samrat Chaudhary was expanded on Thursday. The JD(U) inducted three new leaders into the cabinet, including Nishant Kumar, Bulo Mandal, and Shweta Gupta.

articles.title: Global markets: AI and chip boom fuel expansion of trillion-dollar club
articles.description: Currently, 13 companies worldwide are valued at more than $1 trillion. Of these, 10 are from the US, with Taiwan, South Korea, and Saudi Arabia contributing one each. An additional 13 companies globally sit at the half-trillion-dollar mark, nine of them American, and one each from China, the Netherlands, South Korea, and Mexico.

articles.title: Puducherry Assembly Election Results 2026 at a glance: Vote share, seats tally and more
articles.description: The NDA alliance won the Puducherry Assembly elections on May 4, with the incumbent AINRC coming back to power with the help of its allies. Here are graphics giving an overview of the results.

articles.title: ‘Take lessons from Bengal’: Maharashtra BJP targets 50 lakh ‘ineligible’ voters ahead of SIR
articles.description: At a Mumbai meeting, two BJP leaders stressed that party workers should use the SIR to take its anti-Bangladeshi infiltration drive to its logical end.

articles.title: Vendors accuse Bhooth Bangla makers of Rs 30 lakh non-payment; Balaji denies allegations
articles.description: Bhooth Bangla has landed in controversy after vendors accused the makers of unpaid dues, including an alleged pending payment of Rs 30 lakh. The film continues to perform strongly at the ticket window.

articles.title: ‘While I do shed crocodile tears, I was left with no other option’, says Mehli Mistry on vote against Srinivasan
articles.description: Mehli Mistry defended his decision to oppose trustee reappointments ahead of the Tata Sons board meeting discussing governance and representation issues.

articles.title: “We Will Outlast Them”: Prof. Marandi Warns U.S. Of A Long War; Slams UAE For Aiding Ground Invasion
articles.description: From the heart of Tehran, India Today speaks with Professor Mohammad Marandi on the escalating 39-day-old conflict. Marandi asserts that despite heavy damage to Iranian petrochemical and pharmaceutical infrastructure, the leadership remains unified. He characterizes the U.S. "maximum pressure" and naval blockade as a global economic catastrophe that Iran is prepared to outwait. Marandi specifically warns neighboring regimes, like the UAE, that hosting U.S. ground troops and drones makes them direct participants in the war. Defiant and resolute, he emphasises that for Iran, this is a "fight for survival" that they are destined to win.

articles.title: 9 terror camps, 11 airfields, 13 aircraft: India lists Pakistan losses on Op Sindoor anniversary
articles.description: When asked about losses on the Indian side, Air Marshal AK Bharti said, “Neither has any of our military infrastructure been damaged, nor civilian structure.”

articles.title: Questioning religious practices will break religion and civilisation: Supreme Court in Dawoodi Bohras case
articles.description: Supreme Court warns that questioning religious practices could disrupt religion and civilization, amid hearings on women's discrimination in faith.

articles.title: Many MLAs with Venugopal, party workers back Satheesan: In race for Kerala CM post, AICC assesses options
articles.description: Ramesh Chennithala is another leader in contention, and his camp is confident his seniority will be taken into account.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Marcus Aurelius: ‘Never let the future disturb you; you will meet it, if you...’ - Why worrying too much about the future steals peace from the present, explained by the Stoic philosopher
articles.description: Marcus Aurelius Quote of the Day suggests that fear of the future often creates more suffering than the actual challenges themselves. In his famous words from Meditations, the Stoic philosopher reminds people that the same reason, strength, and resilience helping them survive todays struggles will also help them face tomorrows uncertainties. It encourages people to stop overthinking the future and focus on facing the present.

articles.title: TVK confident of forming govt in Tamil Nadu, Vijay to be CM forever
articles.description: TVK Fisheries Wing Secretary Ganeshan expressed confidence in forming the govt in Tamil Nadu under actor Vijay's leadership after winning 108 seats. The party is 10 short of a majority, but Congress has extended support to the new formation.

articles.title: Dharmendra Pradhan slams TMC over killing of Suvendu Adhikari's aide
articles.description: Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan condemned the killing of Chandranath Rath, aide to Suvendu Adhikari, blaming TMC for a 'blood-soaked political culture.' He called it a 'barbaric execution' and part of a deliberate 'ecosystem of terror.'

articles.title: 'Avatar': James Cameron sued over unauthorized use of actress's face
articles.description: Actress Q'orianka Kilcher has sued James Cameron and Disney, alleging her face was used without consent at age 14 to create the 'Avatar' character Neytiri. The suit claims her likeness was extracted from a photo and used across the franchise.

articles.title: BJP slams TMC's 'Jungle Raj' after Suvendu Adhikari's PA killed
articles.description: BJP's Shehzad Poonawalla alleged political violence is in TMC's "DNA" after the killing of Suvendu Adhikari's aide. He accused TMC of creating a "Jungle Raj" in West Bengal and targeting BJP workers, citing post-poll violence.

articles.title: A Jhalmuri Comeback In Bengal Oath After PM's Viral Grassroots Message
articles.description: Jhalmuri is now set to be a part of the celebrations during the swearing-in ceremony of the newly elected cabinet of the BJP government in West Bengal.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari's PA murder: BJP leader says aide's killing 'pre-planned'
articles.description: West Bengal BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari has called the killing of his personal assistant a "cold-blooded and pre-planned murder." He urged party workers to maintain peace while assuring that the culprits will be brought to justice. Union Minister Sukanta Majumdar echoed these sentiments, stating that the police investigation is progressing and those responsible will not be spared.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari calls aide’s killing ‘pre-planned’: 'Result of 15 years of Mahajungle Raj'

articles.title: Donald Trump says deal with Iran possible, claims military decimated
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said a deal with Iran is 'very possible' following positive talks, adding that their military has been decimated. Negotiations are reportedly advancing on a framework for a ceasefire and broader nuclear talks.

articles.title: Iran wants to negotiate, make a deal, says Trump; no nukes priority
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said Iran wants to negotiate a deal on the West Asia crisis. He stressed that preventing Iran from getting a nuclear weapon is a top priority and that the US is assessing whether any agreement would be satisfactory.

articles.title: Panchkula: Congress alleges over 8,500 duplicate votes in MC electoral roll

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari's assistant shot dead 2 days after BJP's mega Bengal victory
articles.description: In a shocking development, a close aide of West Bengal BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari was shot dead in Madhyamgram area near Kolkata on Wednesday night, triggering tension in the locality and prompting a police investigation.The victim has been identified as Chandra Nath Dutt, personal assistant to Adhikari. According to initial inputs, the incident involved an exchange of fire in which at least four rounds were reportedly fired before Chandra was fatally shot.

Reports claim the White House has sent Tehran a one-page memorandum outlining conditions for ending hostilities and setting a framework for nuclear negotiations.

articles.title: Tripura CPIM Brainstorms Revival Strategy After Electoral Setbacks
articles.description: CPIM State Committee Meeting Agartala: The Tripura unit of the CPIM began a crucial two-day State Committee meeting here today. Led by State Secretary Jitendra Choudhury, the party is analyzing recent losses in the TTAADC and Dharmanagar polls. The deliberations will focus on the party’s revival strategy after the election debacle in both.
